key_points: The Suites are a coveted dorm; time slot determines your chances
Move-in described as chaotic but manageable with right mindset
Limited housing options mean early action on selection matters
key_quotes: 'I was hoping to get in the suites. I'm not sure if you mentioned it but what was your time slot for picking housing?'
'but whose life isn't in shambles when they move in?' — sophomore returning student
'I move in next Tuesday and I honestly was really nervous but watching this calmed my nerves'

key_points: Greek life dominates campus culture; non-D9 students can feel invisible
Sisterhood is real but cliques form quickly around legacy and wealth
AUC access (Morehouse, Clark Atlanta) expands social scene significantly
key_quotes: 'it seem like clark is the better choice — its cheaper, all the same events really and spelman and morehouse is like the cousin'
'new hair, cau vs morehouse, makeup content, homework/quiz' — typical weekend life
'I don't like how much HBCUs center fraternities and sororities... What about SGA, debate team, other orgs?'

key_points: No specific dining complaints surfaced; not a topic students volunteered
Campus life vlogs show typical college food routines without major praise
Limited data — absence of complaints may simply reflect lack of discussion
key_quotes: No direct dining quotes available from this data set
Students focused on social and financial concerns far more than food
Jackson State's dining hall expansion praised elsewhere — no Spelman equivalent mentioned

key_points: Academic pressure crushes students who were top of class in high school
Culture shift toward only recognizing top students worsens anxiety for many
Mental health counseling has been sought by students since at least 1984
key_quotes: 'Many women who were used to being the brightest and the best all the way through school could not adjust to becoming average at Spelman'
'over the years, I have seen the change where only top students are acknowledged. It was NOT that way when I was there'
'I got deferred last night so my spirits aren't the highest right now, but im gonna do what i have to do to get in'

key_points: Spelman notoriously stingy with financial aid, especially for middle-class families
Students apply to scholarships 'religiously' just to afford tuition
Nobody pays full sticker price — but net cost still shocks families
key_quotes: 'Spelman offered me $0. Tuskegee offered me a full ride. Hampton offered me a full ride. Apply everywhere!'
'This is amazing. Spelman gives little to no aid to middle class families, I assume based on low endowment funds purposed for it'
'girlllaaa this tuition!!! i been applying to scholarships religiously tho, so fingers crossed'

key_points: Academically rigorous — high school habits will not survive freshman year
Business is NOT a direct Spelman major; must cross-register at Morehouse
Graduation rate of 75% is highest among HBCUs — real academic achievement
key_quotes: 'As a freshman be ready to challenge your study skills. What might have gotten you by in high school may not in college'
'you can study business at Morehouse but I think you have to apply for that because it isn't a direct major at Spelman'
'Seven women just made history as co-valedictorians at Spelman' — academic excellence recognized

key_points: No major administrative scandals surfaced specific to Spelman leadership
Alumni actively pressure administration on culture and student recognition issues
Admissions process is opaque; deferrals common even for strong applicants
key_quotes: 'Alumnae are aware of this change in culture/climate and are addressing it with the powers that be'
'I got deferred last night' — common experience even among qualified applicants
'When do you all think decisions will come out? Do you think it'll be the 31st this year?' — admissions timeline frustrates applicants

key_points: Atlanta location is a genuine asset — career access, culture, AUC consortium
Campus itself is historically significant and visually beautiful
Being in Atlanta means access to Morehouse, Clark Atlanta events seamlessly
key_quotes: 'We in Atlanta born raised so I rather them be at home' — local parent perspective
'spelman and morehouse is like the cousin' — AUC proximity makes it feel like one campus
'Spelman College's first graduating class' — deep historical roots visible on campus

key_points: Spelman brand opens doors — alumni network and prestige are real advantages
AUC consortium and Atlanta location create strong internship pipelines
Zahara Jolie's graduation signals ongoing celebrity and cultural cachet
key_quotes: 'HBCUs joyfully prepare their Students through quality educations, to both excel and lead'
'1st generation to college, 1st generation law school and pretty soon, 1st generation doctor' — HBCU trajectory stories are common
'Life After College at an HBCU: Humility goes a very long way. Be proud of your achievements but always remember what you do is not who you are'

key_points: High cost plus weak financial aid makes Spelman a tough value proposition
Peer HBCUs offer full rides to students Spelman accepts with zero aid
'Black elitism' drives families toward Spelman even when cheaper options excel
key_quotes: 'Howard offered me 20k out of 58k. Spelman offered me $0. Tuskegee offered me a full ride'
'Their elitism will hit a brick wall when they see that lack of a financial aid package'
'Please stop asking seniors to only apply to Spelman and Howard — Black elitism has too many parents in a chokehold'

key_points: Sisterhood culture is genuine and emotionally powerful for most students
Prestige-consciousness creates class tension between rich legacies and first-gen students
Dream-school energy is intense — applicants are emotionally invested before arriving
key_quotes: 'It's been my dream for three years to attend Spelman and I'm still working on that dream'
'Everybody is Jack and Jill, entire family D9, 4th generation — it makes me not want to be here'
'I just got into Spelman tonight. This has always been my dream school!!!! I am so excited to meet my future Spelman sisters!!!'

key_points: **Financial Aid:** Spelman routinely offers zero dollars to admitted students
**Class Culture:** Wealthy legacy students dominate social hierarchy visibly
**Academic Pressure:** Culture of only recognizing top students fuels mental health crises
key_quotes: 'Spelman offered me $0. Tuskegee offered me a full ride. Apply everywhere!'
'Everybody is Jack and Jill, entire family D9, 4th generation — these types of schools make you feel like if you're not a rich kid you shouldn't even be there'
'Many women who were used to being the brightest and the best could not adjust to becoming average at Spelman'

key_points: Women's Studies major allows customizable focus tracks not widely advertised
AUC cross-registration lets you take classes at Morehouse and Clark Atlanta
Spelman's 75% graduation rate is highest among all HBCUs — quiet proof of retention
key_quotes: 'once you said you can choose women's studies and choose a focus i was so relieved' — flexibility students don't know about
'you could study business at Morehouse but I think you have to apply for that because it isn't a direct major at Spelman' — cross-registration is the workaround
'Spelman was the highest at 75%' — graduation rate outperforms many PWIs quietly
